summary: The Check Changelog Entry job failed because the CI emitted an error stating the PR would target v1.16 and requires an addition (likely a changelog entry).
root_cause: CI reported: "Currently this PR would target a v1.16 release. Please add ...", indicating the PR needs an added changelog entry or an adjusted release target.

evidence.quote: 2026-04-23T09:06:25.3001661Z ##[error]Currently this PR would target a v1.16 release. Please add ...
evidence.reason: This is the explicit error emitted by the CI that caused the job to fail.
The warnings reference a changelog-related comment string, supporting that the check enforces changelog content.
Shows the presence of at least one error entry in the log summary for this job.

suggested_fix: Add the required changelog entry to the PR (or adjust the PR's release target) so the check no longer reports "Currently this PR would target a v1.16 release. Please add ...".

summary: Dependabot update job failed because a container failed to run during the updater step, causing the updater to report an error and abort.
root_cause: A container failed to start/run during the Dependabot updater: "Failure running container 4d986eac50248057b7f3fa031163542ffb36990efb...", which caused the updater to error and abort the update.

evidence.reason: Directly shows a container run failure with a container ID, indicating a Docker/container-related failure.

evidence.quote: Dependabot encountered '1' error(s) during execution, please check t...
evidence.reason: Shows Dependabot detected an error during execution, consistent with the container failure causing the job to fail.

evidence.quote: ##[error]Dependabot encountered an error performing the update
evidence.reason: The CI step logged an error-level message that the Dependabot update failed.

evidence.quote: Error: The updater encountered one or more errors.
evidence.reason: Confirms the updater terminated due to errors rather than completing successfully.

suggested_fix: Inspect the logs for container ID 4d986eac50248057b7f3fa031163542ffb36990efb to see why it failed to run, verify the runner has Docker available and functional, and then re-run the Dependabot update.

summary: The documentation build failed because a Python SyntaxError occurred while converting a documentation source file, causing the doc-conversion step to raise and the job to exit with code 1.
root_cause: A Python SyntaxError ('invalid syntax') was raised during the documentation conversion process (resolve_autodoc) for a docs source file (../transformers/docs...), causing the docs build to fail and the job to exit with code 1.

evidence.reason: Shows the type of error that occurred during the run.

evidence.quote: SyntaxError: There was an error when converting ../transformers/docs...
evidence.reason: Indicates the SyntaxError occurred while converting a documentation source file in ../transformers/docs...
Shows the failure happened during the resolve_autodoc step of the doc conversion process.

evidence.quote: raise type(e)(f"There was an error when converting {file} to the...
evidence.reason: Shows the code raised an error specifically when converting a file, tying the SyntaxError to the conversion step.
Confirms the job terminated with a non-zero exit code as a result of the error.

suggested_fix: Open the referenced documentation source file(s) under ../transformers/docs and fix the Python syntax error(s). Re-run the documentation build locally (including the resolve_autodoc step) to reproduce and verify the fix before pushing.

summary: The job failed because a git operation could not access the remote repository URL, causing a command to exit with code 128 and the workflow to fail.
root_cause: Git was unable to access the remote URL 'https://github.com/vercel/next.js/', causing a command to fail with exit code 128 and terminating the job.

evidence.reason: Shows git could not access the remote repository URL, indicating a network or access failure.

evidence.quote: Error: Command failed with exit code 128 (Unknown system error -128)
evidence.reason: Indicates the failing command returned exit code 128, which is commonly from git failures.
Confirms the workflow job terminated with an error as a result of the failing command.

evidence.quote: An error occurred while minifying. The result will not be minified.
evidence.reason: Shows a build step encountered an error (non-fatal for minification) earlier in the log; the primary blocker is the git access error that caused the job to fail.

suggested_fix: Check network connectivity and access from the runner to https://github.com/vercel/next.js/ (e.g., test git clone from the runner), verify any required credentials or tokens are available to the job, then re-run the workflow.

summary: The CI job failed because a test assertion failed during the runtests-parallel step, causing the process to exit with code 1.
root_cause: A test assertion failed: an AssertionError shows an expectation mismatch (message starting with 'Add return type \"Foo<string>\"') during the runtests-parallel run, causing the job to exit with code 1.

evidence.quote: AssertionError: expected 'Add return type \'Foo<string>\'' to e...
evidence.reason: Shows a concrete AssertionError indicating a test expectation mismatch involving the string 'Add return type 'Foo<string>''.
Indicates the runtests-parallel step completed but reported errors, pointing to test failures during that step.
Demonstrates the failing process returned a non-zero exit code, causing the CI job to fail.
Identifies the failing CI task as do-runtests-parallel, linking the assertion failure to that job step.
CI runner reporting that the job completed with a failure exit code, consistent with the test assertion failure.

suggested_fix: Run the failing suite locally using the runtests-parallel / do-runtests-parallel script to capture the full assertion output, then either update the test expectation to match the intended output or fix the code producing the message so the assertion passes; re-run CI.
